channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Deception in democracy: Beware the most common types of election-related scams
item.description: Cybersecurity risks are ramping up as the Nov. 5 U.S. general election approaches. Social media and the internet are used to spread propaganda.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SEC fines cybersecurity giants for downplaying effects of SolarWinds attack
item.description: Despite knowing threat actors had accessed systems and exfiltrated data, several companies released statements downplaying the impact of the attack.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: Meet Capacities, the do-it-all productivity app taking on Notion
item.description: Here’s a tantalizing little paradox to chew over: One of the most interesting up-and-coming productivity apps is also one of the most immediately familiar-feeling—on the surface, at least. The app is called Capacities.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: Anthropic debuts AI agents that save coders extra keystrokes
item.description: Anthropic, a startup backed by Alphabet and Amazon.com, released a pair of updated artificial intelligence models on Tuesday, along with a new capability to autonomously perform computer tasks and save users keystrokes.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: ABBA, Radiohead and The Cure musicians sign AI protest letter against 'unlicensed use' of works
item.description: Musicians from ABBA, Radiohead and The Cure have joined actors and authors in signing a protest letter against the mining of their artistry to build artificial intelligence tools.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Big offshore wind project proposed for New York as other sites are evaluated in 3 states
item.description: Offshore wind energy projects in New York, New Jersey and Maryland are moving forward, as federal regulators examine the proposals and opponents increase their legal challenges to the work.
item.dc:creator: Wayne Parry, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here’s how to stay safe from scams
item.description: As scammers find new ways to steal money and personal information, consumers should be more vigilant about who they trust, especially online.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Agencies prep for next phase of ‘zero trust’ cyber upgrades
item.description: An upcoming deadline will provide a pulse check on the government-wide push toward "zero trust" cybersecurity defenses. The post Agencies prep for next phase of ‘zero trust’ cyber upgrades first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: AI dominates Gartner’s 2025 predictions
item.description: Artificial Intelligence continues to dominate this week’s Gartner IT Symposium/Xpo, as well as the research firm’s annual predictions list.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta again testing facial recognition software to fight 'celeb bait' scams
item.description: Meta, the parent company of Facebook and Instagram, is testing facial recognition software to combat “celeb bait” scams on its platforms, as well as to allow users to more easily regain access to their accounts.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Giant fish thought to be extinct is spotted in the Mekong River
item.description: Scientists say a giant fish in the Mekong River thought to be extinct has been spotted three times in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tech giants press Congress to codify AI Safety Institute
item.description: A coalition of more than 60 tech companies and industry groups, including Amazon, Anthropic, Google, Meta, Microsoft and OpenAI, urged congressional leaders Tuesday to pass legislation permanently authorizing the U.S. Artificial Intelligence Safety Institute by the end of the year.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Cpacket Launches High-Performance Packet Capture Appliance
item.description: Cpacket Networks introduces the cPacket Capture cStor 200S appliance, designed for high-performance environments and delivering metrics for network throughput, capacity, latency, and errors.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Michigan secretary of state pushes back against Musk's voter registration claims
item.description: Michigan Secretary of State Jocelyn Benson (D) and tech billionaire Elon Musk got into a days-long back-and-forth on the social platform X about voter registration in the Wolverine State.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: FTC's rule banning fake online reviews goes into effect
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal rule banning fake online reviews is now in effect. The Federal Trade Commission issued the rule in August banning the sale or purchase of online reviews.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Still using 'LOL'? That could mean you are getting old
item.description: As time has seen several follow-up iterations, including ROFL and LMAO, the original "laugh out loud" acronym is a telltale sign that who you are talking to may be a millennial.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, NewsNation

item.title: One of the world’s largest book publishers adds AI warnings to its books
item.description: To make generative AI tools (genAI) work as well as possible, tech companies have chosen to train their large language models (LLMs) on large amounts of text, even though doing so could run afoul of copyright laws.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Slack researcher discusses the fear, loathing and excitement surrounding AI in the workplace
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Artificial intelligence's recent rise to the forefront of business has left most office workers wondering how often they should use the technology and whether a computer will eventually replace them.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it's the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams. “Scams have become so sophisticated now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA MORGA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Get your nominations in for the Oscars of federal technology transfers
item.description: "Technology's changed quite a bit since the 80s. In 2024, we had 32 award winners we recognized from eight federal agencies," said Jesse Midgett. The post Get your nominations in for the Oscars of federal technology transfers first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: States grasping the benefits of, need for cyber harmonization
item.description: Public and private sector experts say programs like StateRAMP make their data and networks safer as cyber threats become more serious and resources sparse. The post States grasping the benefits of, need for cyber harmonization first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: AI stagnation: The gap between AI investment and AI adoption
item.description: AI continues to shape cloud strategies, but AI implementation is going slower than most predicted. This is frustrating for technology providers who have made big bets on AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Alabama man arrested in SEC social media account hack that led the price of bitcoin to spike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Alabama man was arrested Thursday for his alleged role in the January hack of a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission social media account that led the price of bitcoin to spike, the Justice Department said. Eric Council Jr.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Global cybersecurity talent gap widens
item.description: Despite some recent positive news for tech hiring, a growing cybersecurity talent shortage threatens to hamstring businesses. Data from CyberSeek shows that in the U.S., some 265,000 more cybersecurity workers are needed to solve current staffing needs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Secure Azure Kubernetes with Advanced Container Networking Services
item.description: Microsoft’s Azure Kubernetes Service is designed to take care of much of the day-to-day management of Kubernetes, simplifying a lot of the complexity that goes with building and running large-scale, cloud-native applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World
